intro: MiCA enforcement makes good today: Binance suspends EU services from midnight as the transitional period expires. Strategy pivots from passive Bitcoin accumulator to active capital manager. AI Research & Models beat is quiet; the news this week was made in prior editions.

items.headline: Copilot's first usage-based billing month closes: agentic workflows confirmed at 5–10x flat-rate costs
items.summary: June 30 marks the end of GitHub Copilot's inaugural token-metered billing cycle, which replaced unlimited access on June 1. Developers running agentic sessions — code review, multi-file edits, parallel agent loops — are seeing confirmed monthly charges of $750–$3,000, versus the $39 Pro+ flat rate. The official community thread accumulated 400-plus comments and nearly 900 downvotes when the switch went live.
items.why: The first full monthly bill landing today gives teams real data to decide whether to cap Copilot usage, switch to OpenCode or Cursor, or absorb the higher cost.

items.headline: Qualcomm closes $3.9B Modular acquisition, giving AI developers Mojo and MAX on Snapdragon
items.summary: Qualcomm confirmed the close of its all-stock acquisition of Modular for $3.92 billion on June 26, gaining roughly 150 engineers, the Mojo programming language (a Python superset built for AI/ML performance), and the MAX graph compiler and inference engine. The MAX stack already runs cross-vendor across Nvidia, AMD, Intel, and Arm hardware; Qualcomm's goal is making its Snapdragon and Dragonfly data-center chips viable AI inference targets without needing CUDA.
items.why: First credible full-stack CUDA alternative with major chip-vendor backing; could meaningfully expand the non-Nvidia hardware surface for AI model deployment.

items.headline: GitHub Copilot for Jira reaches general availability with real-time agent progress streaming
items.summary: GitHub made its Copilot coding agent integration for Jira generally available on June 25, following a March public preview. The GA release adds real-time progress streaming to Jira tickets — status updates appear in the issue as the agent works — plus model selection, Confluence context via MCP, custom agents, and follow-up instruction delivery from the Jira chat panel after the agent opens a draft pull request.
items.why: Closes the loop between issue tracking and autonomous code generation in a single workflow, without leaving the project management tool.

items.headline: Binance suspends EU services as MiCA enforcement arrives: world's largest exchange locked out of Europe
items.summary: Effective July 1, Binance is halting new orders, deposits, sign-ups, and staking products for EU residents after failing to secure a CASP license under the EU's Markets in Crypto-Assets regulation. The exchange withdrew its Greek application on June 24 ahead of an expected rejection citing fit-and-proper concerns around co-founder Changpeng Zhao and historical AML penalties; it now plans to seek authorization in France. Existing user funds remain safe and withdrawable.
items.why: The world's largest crypto exchange by volume is removed from the European market on MiCA's first enforcement day — the most significant regulatory market exit in crypto since the GDPR era.

items.headline: Strategy unveils Digital Credit Capital Framework, authorizes up to $1.25B in Bitcoin sales
items.summary: MicroStrategy (now Strategy) announced a 'Digital Credit Capital Framework' on June 29, departing from its passive accumulation model. The framework authorizes $2 billion in stock repurchases and a Bitcoin monetization program allowing up to $1.25 billion in BTC sales to fund preferred dividends and debt repurchases. The company still holds approximately 843,738 BTC; it made its first Bitcoin sale in company history in late May (32 BTC).
items.why: Strategy treating its ~$50B BTC stack as a flexible capital resource — not a one-way accumulation trade — marks a structural shift in the single largest corporate Bitcoin holder's posture.

items.headline: White House sets July 22 deadline for US Strategic Bitcoin Reserve architecture
items.summary: White House Digital Assets Advisory Council executive director Bo Hines confirmed the Trump administration will release the design for a US Strategic Bitcoin Reserve before a July 22 inter-agency regulatory report deadline. The US currently holds roughly 328,000 BTC (~$20B) via law-enforcement seizures. The reserve design will determine whether the government actively acquires additional Bitcoin or simply retains existing holdings under a formal custody framework.
items.why: July 22 is the first concrete policy gate that could make the US a systematic Bitcoin buyer — outcome will materially affect long-term supply dynamics and institutional positioning.
